content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
:File 
From 
: 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Ted 
Date :April 5, 1983 
Subiect: Telephone Call from Vance Raye on March 31, 1983 
Vance Rays counsel to the Governor, called to ask whether 
there were any problems with a member of a state board or 
commission also being a lobbyist. 
I consulted with Bob Leidigh 
and provided Vance with the following advice. 
1. 
A lobbyist may not serve as a member of the Fair 
Political Practices Commission. 
2. 
The board member would have to disqualify himself 
from any decision in which there was a "nexus It between the 
governmental decision and the purpose for which he received 
income as a lobbyist. 
See regulation 18702 (b) (3) (B) . 
3. 
The board member would have to disqualify himself 
from "using his official position to influence" any decision 
in which he had a financial interest.

sections.question_synthetic: Can a member of a state board or commission also serve as a lobbyist, and what disqualification requirements apply?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A lobbyist cannot serve on the FPPC itself. Board members who are lobbyists must disqualify themselves from decisions with a nexus to their lobbying work and from using their official position to influence decisions in which they have a financial interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal memorandum documenting telephone advice rather than formal letter response. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections; advice provided as numbered points. Document identifies caller as 'Vance Raye' (likely Vance Raye) and references regulation 18702(b)(3)(B).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a member of a state board or commission also serve as a lobbyist, and what disqualification requirements apply?

CONCLUSION: A lobbyist cannot serve on the FPPC itself. Board members who are lobbyists must disqualify themselves from decisions with a nexus to their lobbying work and from using their official position to influence decisions in which they have a financial interest.

I consulted with Bob Leidigh and provided Vance with the following advice. 1. A lobbyist may not serve as a member of the Fair Political Practices Commission. 2. The board member would have to disqualify himself from any decision in which there was a "nexus It between the governmental decision and the purpose for which he received income as a lobbyist. See regulation 18702 (b) (3) (B) . 3. The board member would have to disqualify himself from "using his official position to influence" any decision in which he had a financial interest.
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum documenting advice given via telephone to Governor's Counsel regarding the permissibility of dual service as both a state board/commission member and a lobbyist, with three key conclusions about disqualification requirements.
